data.title: OTC Markets outlines strategic expansion with OTCID launch and 11% revenue growth
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: OTC Markets Group Inc. (OTCM) Q2 2025

Management View

  • President and CEO Robert Cromwell Coulson reported that "gross revenues grew 11% and net revenues by 10% during the second quarter" with each business line contributing to the growth: "19% in OTC Link, 14% in Market Data and 3% in Corporate Services."
  • Coulson highlighted the ongoing buildout of the overnight trading business, stating, "Both the Market Data team and our OTC Link Group continues to build out the foundation of our overnight trading business, working with current and prospective subscribers around the globe."
  • He also announced the successful launch of the OTCID basic market on July 1, emphasizing its role in expanding offerings to a wider range of companies and increasing engagement: "OTCID now expands our offering to a wider range of companies and its introduction has created increased interest in all of our markets."
  • The CEO stressed the importance of sustained regulatory focus, noting, "The regulatory and legislative environment is extraordinarily active. The SEC, FINRA and lawmakers on Capitol Hill are soliciting ideas and taking action on issues ranging from capital formation to market structure to digital assets."
  • Coulson announced, "on August 5, our Board of Directors declared a quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share, payable on September 18."
  • CFO Antonia Georgieva congratulated the team for the OTCID launch and reported, "We generated $30.5 million in gross revenues, up 11% compared to the prior year period."

Outlook

  • Management stated continued focus on overnight trading and the newly launched OTCID basic market as key strategic priorities to drive future engagement and growth.
  • Coulson indicated that competitive dynamics are expected to intensify in overnight trading: "Overnight trading in NMS securities is a competitive market, and we expect more entrants into this space over time, including the national securities exchanges."
  • The company plans to provide additional OTCID metrics and trends in subsequent quarters.

Financial Results

  • Georgieva reported "OTC Link's gross revenues increased 19%, driven by a 40% increase in transaction-based revenues from OTC Link ECN and OTC Link NQB as we benefited from a higher number of shares traded on those platforms."
  • She noted "revenues from our market data licensing business increased 14% quarter-over-quarter, reflecting a 20% increase in redistributor-based revenues and 16% increase in revenues from direct sold licenses."
  • Corporate Services revenues increased 3% in the second quarter, with DNS revenue up 13% and an uptick in new companies added to OTCQX and OTCQB.
  • Operating expenses rose 8% to $18.4 million, primarily due to higher compensation, consulting, and IT costs.
  • Operating profit margin was 29.9%, and diluted earnings per share increased to $0.60. Adjusted EBITDA was $11.1 million. Cash provided by operating activities was $11.2 million.
  • The company returned $2.2 million to investors in dividends during the quarter.

Q&A

  • Steven Silver, Argus Research: Asked about drivers behind strong growth in international issuers on OTCQX and OTCQB. Coulson replied, "the primary driver was OTCID and really making clear to investors which companies are connected into the market and which ones are not. A secondary driver is we removed the sponsor requirement for companies to join."
  • Silver also inquired about EDGAR subscription trends. Coulson responded that efforts are underway to integrate SEDAR filings and enhance data products, with plans to "add some sales and client support resources to start going out to that customer base and pushing new ideas and cross-selling."
  • Unidentified Analyst (Sidoti): Sought feedback on OTCID customer reception. Coulson explained initial hesitation is turning to interest, especially as companies see peers adopting: "Our goal is really to drive companies to provide the highest level of information that they can for their situation in a manner that is efficient and shows up on investor screens and in broker-dealer machines."
  • The analyst also asked about OTCID revenue contribution and trends. Georgieva clarified, "Subscribers that have had in the past existing relationship with us, about 2/3 of them chose to subscribe to DNS during the 6 months in anticipation of the July 1 deadline."
  • Further question on conversion rate from PINK to OTCID. General Counsel Daniel Zinn emphasized tracking trends by monitoring numbers in reported metrics, indicating a more nuanced transition than a simple conversion rate.
  • Walter Hopkins, 18 Square: Asked about potential for operating margins to rise to exchange-like levels if revenues double. Coulson responded, "I am not margin obsessed. I am margin of safety obsessed...Our goal is to be the kind of company that Charlie Munger would want to own."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed for clarity on the impact of strategic initiatives, future margin potential, and customer adoption, demonstrating a neutral to slightly positive tone with an emphasis on specifics and follow-up questions.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in prepared remarks and the Q&A, consistently reiterating strategic priorities and long-term focus, with phrases such as "our goal is really to drive companies" and "we will be there to serve our subscribers."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst sentiment shifted to a more granular focus on the effectiveness of the OTCID launch and international issuer growth, while management displayed increased confidence, especially regarding initial successes of OTCID and overnight trading.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue growth accelerated from 10% in Q1 to 11% in Q2, with a notable transition to positive growth in Corporate Services revenues after a decrease in Q1.
  • OTCID launch shifted from preparation to execution, with immediate impact on subscriber engagement and product metrics, compared to Q1's anticipation.
  • Operating expenses increased at a slightly higher rate in Q2, but the profit margin also improved versus Q1.
  • Management's tone evolved from cautious optimism about upcoming initiatives in Q1 to stronger confidence following tangible results in Q2.
  • Analysts' focus evolved from broad questions about strategic initiatives and price changes to more targeted inquiries about adoption rates, competitive dynamics, and operational leverage.

Risks and Concerns

  • Coulson acknowledged unpredictability in trading volumes, stating, "We cannot control overall market volumes or predict economic events."
  • Georgieva echoed concerns about future volatility in nonprofessional user revenues due to internal policy changes at a key subscriber, which resulted in a "21% reduction in reported nonprofessional users."
  • Management cautioned that "trading volumes remain highly unpredictable and could decline in the future."
  • The regulatory environment was described as "extraordinarily active," with pending actions from the SEC, FINRA, and lawmakers potentially impacting operations.

Final Takeaway

OTC Markets Group delivered strong double-digit revenue growth in the second quarter, supported by robust performance across business lines and the successful launch of the OTCID basic market. Key strategic initiatives in overnight trading and issuer engagement are gaining traction, while management remains focused on operational integrity and navigating an evolving regulatory landscape. The company continues to emphasize sustainable growth, prudent cost management, and long-term value creation for shareholders.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Avidbank prices $60M IPO
data.description:
• Avidbank (OTC:AVBH) has priced its initial public offering at $23.00 per share, offering 2.61 million shares of common stock.
  • The shares are expected to begin trading on the Nasdaq Global Select Market on August 8, 2025, under the ticker symbol "AVBH". The IPO is expected to close on August 11, 2025, pending standard closing conditions.
  • The company has also granted underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to 391,500 additional shares at the IPO price, excluding discounts and commissions.
  • Proceeds from the offering will be used to strengthen the capital of Avidbank, support organic growth, expand lending, improve regulatory capital, potentially restructure its securities portfolio, and for general corporate purposes.

data.title: BioVie stock dips on pricing $12M securities offering
data.description:
BioVie (NASDAQ:BIVI) has priced a public offering of 6 million units at $2.00 per unit, raising approximately $12 million in gross proceeds.

Each unit includes one share of common stock and one warrant, or pre-funded units in lieu thereof, with each pre-funded unit consisting of one pre-funded warrant and one warrant.

The warrants are immediately exercisable at $2.50 per share and expire five years from issuance. Each pre-funded warrant will be immediately exercisable, will entitle the holder to purchase one share of common stock and may be exercised at any time until exercised in full.

The warrants (BIVIW) are expected to begin trading on Nasdaq on August 8, 2025. The common stock and warrants must be bought together but will be issued separately.

BioVie has also given underwriters a 45-day option to purchase up to 900,000 additional securities to cover any over-allotments.

Proceeds will be used for working capital and general corporate purposes. The offering is expected to close on August 11, 2025, subject to customary conditions.

BIVI fell 7.9% premarket on Friday.

data.title: Cosco seeks at least 20% stake in $23 billion Panama Canal ports deal - report
data.description:
China's state-owned shipping giant Cosco (OTCPK:CICOF) reportedly aims to secure at least a 20%-30% stake in a $23 billion ports deal involving strategic assets in the Panama Canal, as Beijing moves to renegotiate the terms of a sale previously praised by U.S. President Donald Trump.

The Chinese group is demanding a large stake after an exclusive negotiating window for BlackRock (BLK) and Swiss-Italian shipping group MSC expired, the Financial Times reported on Friday.

The two companies were originally set to form a consortium to buy 43 ports, including two in Panama, from Hong Kong billionaire Li Ka-shing’s CK Hutchison.

Under one of the options being discussed, Cosco would receive a stake in 41 ports, but not the two Panama ports that Trump has alleged are subject to Chinese influence, the report said.

Two people familiar with Beijing’s view said Cosco was the only Chinese company permitted to be involved in the talks, giving it immense negotiating power over BlackRock and MSC because they would need a Chinese partner to get approval from the State Administration for Market Regulation.

data.title: Southwest Gas prices Centuri share sale at $19.50/share
data.description:
Southwest Gas (NYSE:SWX) has priced a secondary public offering of 15 million shares of Centuri Holdings (NYSE:CTRI) at $19.50 per share.

The company is the sole selling shareholder and has also granted underwriters a 30-day option to buy up to an additional 2.25 million shares. The offering is expected to close on August 11, 2025, pending standard closing conditions.

Separately, Southwest Gas will sell 1.57 million shares of Centuri stock to Carl Icahn’s investment firms — Icahn Partners and Icahn Partners Master Fund — through a private placement at the same $19.50 price.

CTRI fell 7% after-hours on Thursday.

data.title: Alamo Group outlines continued industrial growth and M&A acceleration as net debt nears zero
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Alamo Group Inc. (ALG) Q2 2025

Management View

  • Jeffery Leonard, President and CEO, highlighted "strong solid operating performance, driven by sustained strength in the governmental and industrial markets, supported by further modest improvement in the markets for our vegetation management equipment." He emphasized that operating income improved significantly due to "efficiency improvement measures we have successfully implemented over the past several quarters."
  • Leonard pointed to robust demand in the government and industrial contractor segments, with sales in the Industrial Equipment division up nearly 18% year-over-year, all of it organic. "Sales of excavators, vacuum trucks, street sweepers, highway safety vehicles and snow removal equipment all improved nicely."
  • The CEO reported that the order backlog in Industrial Equipment stood at nearly $510 million, providing "very good visibility and confidence through the second half of the year and into the early months of 2026."
  • Leonard announced the completion of the Ring-O-Matic acquisition, stating it "nicely complements our vacuum truck and excavator product line and offers potential to accelerate growth in our equipment rental business."
  • Agnieszka K. Kamps, EVP and CFO, stated: "Second quarter of 2025 revenue was $419.1 million compared to strong prior year second quarter revenue of $416.3 million. Gross profit for the quarter was $108.3 million with a margin of 25.8% of net sales compared to $108.2 million and margin of 26% for the same period last year."
  • Kamps added, "SG&A expenses were $57.1 million, which is a reduction of 6% driven by savings in Vegetation Management division. Operating income in the second quarter of 2025 was $47.1 million with an operating margin of 11.2% of net sales, reflecting an increase of 83 basis points compared to the second quarter in 2024."
  • Kamps also noted that net debt was reduced to $11.3 million, a 93.5% improvement from the previous year.

Outlook

  • Leonard stated, "We remain optimistic about the company's prospects for the next several quarters and beyond." He pointed to sustained strength in Industrial Equipment, further recovery in Vegetation Management, improving efficiencies, and a lower administrative cost structure as supporting positive developments.
  • Management reported that the Industrial Equipment division's backlog and order bookings provide confidence "through the second half of the year and into the early months of 2026."
  • Leonard expressed optimism about the "increasingly active M&A pipeline," enabled by the company's net debt approaching zero.

Financial Results

  • Consolidated revenue for Q2 2025 was $419.1 million, up from $416.3 million in Q2 2024.
  • Gross profit for the quarter was $108.3 million, with a gross margin of 25.8%.
  • Operating income for the quarter was $47.1 million, with an operating margin of 11.2%.
  • Net income for the second quarter was $31.1 million or $2.57 per diluted share, compared to $28.3 million or $2.35 per diluted share last year.
  • SG&A expenses declined 6% year-over-year.
  • Interest expense decreased by $2.4 million compared to the same period in 2024.
  • Vegetation Management division reported net sales of $178.4 million, a 15.7% year-over-year decline, but an 8.8% sequential improvement.
  • Industrial Equipment division net sales were $240.7 million, representing 17.6% organic growth.
  • Operating income for Industrial Equipment was $34.3 million, or 14.3% of net sales.
  • Operating cash flow year-to-date was $36.9 million.

Q&A

  • Christopher Paul Moore, CJS Securities: Asked about Vegetation Management order trends and outlook for Q4. Leonard responded that the trend is likely to continue, "The climb out has to be consistent, and I think it will be... I'm encouraged. The markets were a little bit better in that space than I thought."
  • Moore queried about tariffs and inflation risks. Leonard said, "We've largely mitigated that risk by shifting a good deal of that production from Canada into the U.S... the pressure on our purchase prices has been far less significant than I think Agnes and I anticipated."
  • Moore asked about capacity constraints in Industrial. Leonard replied, "We're not feeling too much pressure yet... labor is the bigger constraint again."
  • Mircea Dobre, Baird: Asked about Vegetation Management revenue trajectory and seasonality. Leonard said, "I think revenue will continue to build, but very slowly in that division."
  • Dobre inquired about margin progression and facility consolidation costs. Leonard responded, "I wouldn't factor in too much of a margin improvement for the next quarter at least."
  • Dobre asked about CEO succession. Leonard said, "The process is going exactly according to the plan, and I think you're going to see it reach a conclusion in the third quarter."
  • Michael Shlisky, D.A. Davidson: Asked about capital allocation and new product categories. Leonard said, "Capital allocation remains very heavily toward M&A at the moment."
  • Shlisky queried about the snow business outlook. Leonard stated, "There seems to be plenty of legs left in that market from my point of view, Mike, I don't see any kind of a slowdown coming there."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts displayed a neutral to slightly positive tone, focusing on order trends, margin improvements, capital allocation, and strategic direction, with questions often seeking clarity on sustainability of trends and risks.
  • Management remained optimistic and confident, with Leonard stating, "We remain optimistic about the company's prospects for the next several quarters and beyond," and describing the M&A pipeline as "increasingly active."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management maintained a constructive tone, with management’s confidence slightly more pronounced and analysts seeking confirmation of positive trends and risk mitigation.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language remains optimistic, with enhanced emphasis on M&A acceleration and net debt reduction.
  • Strategic focus shifts toward leveraging an "increasingly active M&A pipeline" and integrating the Ring-O-Matic acquisition.
  • Key metrics improved sequentially: Industrial Equipment revenue and backlog up, Vegetation Management division showing signs of steady recovery, and further SG&A reductions.
  • Management’s tone reflects increased confidence in operational efficiency and market outlook, while analysts persist in probing for details on execution and risk.
  • CEO succession process advanced further this quarter, with a resolution targeted for Q3.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited ongoing risks from tariffs, noting they have largely mitigated exposure by shifting production from Canada to the U.S.
  • Labor constraints are emerging as a potential bottleneck, particularly in the Industrial division.
  • Demand in Vegetation Management remains under pressure, with cautious dealer sentiment and exposure to interest rate developments.
  • Management is monitoring order cancellations in forestry, and notes that unfavorable product mix may challenge near-term margins.

Final Takeaway

Alamo Group delivered solid performance in Q2 2025, led by robust Industrial Equipment sales and improving operational efficiencies. The company’s strong backlog, active acquisition pipeline, and net debt approaching zero position it for further growth and strategic expansion. While risks persist in tariffs, labor, and Vegetation Management recovery, management’s confidence in continued improvement and disciplined capital allocation underscores its forward-looking strategy for the remainder of the year and beyond.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Manulife Investment Management names June Chua as Head of Asian Equities
data.description:
• Manulife Investment Management on Thursday announced the appointment of June Chua as Head of Asian Equities, effective August 4.
  • Based in Hong Kong, June will also serve as Head of Asian Equity Research, coordinating research coverage and resources across the region.
  • June will report to Charlie Dutton, Head of Emerging Market Equities.
  • Manulife Investment Management also announced that Yuting Shao has joined as  Senior Global Macro Strategist as a part of its multi-asset solutions team.

data.title: Genie Energy confirms $40M-$50M adjusted EBITDA guidance as solar and retail units diverge amid margin pressure
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Genie Energy Ltd. (GNE) Q2 2025

Management View

  • Michael M. Stein, CEO, stated that the second quarter delivered "solid operational progress and double-digit top line growth," but noted that "our bottom line was impacted by significant margin compression at GRE, which weighed on our bottom line results." He highlighted GRE's expansion to approximately 419,000 meters served, with churn dropping to 4.8% from 5.5% in the first quarter, and a 15% year-over-year increase in meters and a 20% rise in RCEs. Stein described the quarter as being affected by "wholesale power price increases in some of the supply markets, most notably within the PJM and MISO interconnection zones," mainly due to policy concerns and warmer-than-usual weather.
  • Stein reported "very strong results" at GREW, with a 44% revenue increase and the segment approaching breakeven. At Diversegy, revenue grew over 50% year-over-year and profitability increased by almost 3,000%. Genie Solar revenue jumped over 6x to $1 billion, with the bottom line loss decreasing by 90% and significant SG&A reductions. Stein also announced the expected commissioning of the Lansing Community Solar project in the third quarter and shared that "we have paused work on several of the earliest stage development pipeline projects to reevaluate their economics" due to changes in federal tax and budget legislation impacting solar incentives. He added, "because of the legislation, we are pausing efforts to add projects to our development pipeline."
  • Stein confirmed, "we are expecting GRE's margins to return closer to historical levels" and reiterated full year consolidated adjusted EBITDA guidance of $40 million to $50 million.
  • Avi Goldin, CFO, stated, "Consolidated revenue in the second quarter increased 16% to $105.3 million, driven by growth at both Genie Retail and Genie Renewables." Goldin detailed that consolidated gross profit decreased 30% to $23.5 million, gross margin dropped 1,400 basis points to 22%, and income from operations was $2 million with adjusted EBITDA of $3 million, primarily due to "the reduced gross profit at GRE."

Outlook

  • Management reaffirmed, "we are expecting GRE's margins to return closer to historical levels. Assuming a normalized commodity environment and with continued improvement in growth at GREW, we confirm Genie's 2025 consolidated adjusted EBITDA guidance at $40 million to $50 million."
  • Stein stated, "we are well positioned for the remainder of the year and expect to meet our full adjusted EBITDA guidance of $40 million to $50 million, assuming normalized weather conditions."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, guidance remains unchanged.

Financial Results

  • Goldin reported, "Consolidated revenue in the second quarter increased 16% to $105.3 million," with GRE revenue up 14% to $99 million. Electricity revenue totaled $89.9 million and natural gas revenue was $9.1 million. GREW revenue increased 57% to $6.3 million.
  • Consolidated gross profit fell to $23.5 million, and gross margin was 22%. Income from operations was $2 million and adjusted EBITDA was $3 million, both down from the previous year.
  • GRE saw income from operations decrease to $4 million and adjusted EBITDA to $4.4 million. GREW's loss from operations narrowed to $181,000, with adjusted EBITDA improving to negative $97,000.
  • Net income attributable to Genie common stockholders was $2.8 million or $0.11 per share.
  • Goldin also noted share repurchases of approximately 159,000 shares and a quarterly dividend payment of $0.075 per share.

Q&A

  • Scott, Private Investor: "What gives you hope or confidence that your retail margins or rather your wholesale margins will return to normal? Obviously, the world has changed quite a bit over the last 6 months, and this is a relatively tough quarter for Genie. So maybe starting with that one, what gives you hope that your margins will return close to normal and reaffirming your guidance for the year?" Unidentified Company Representative: "Our margins were hurt in the quarter by definitely some political factors, but I think mostly by weather... We think things are starting to calm down on the wholesale front, and that's what gives us confidence that we should be able to pull off our guidance."
  • Scott, Private Investor: "Are there any hedging strategies or trading strategies you could take and hope on weather is not a great strategy." Unidentified Company Representative: "We hedge our business at a very high percentage... there is some that is definitely out of our control. But by and large, the vast majority of our power is already purchased."
  • Scott: "How does weather impact your margins so significantly if you're materially hedged... amount make that big a difference?" Unidentified Company Representative: "If weather is significantly different than historicals, then even that, call it, 15% to 20% can make a very big difference in the margins."
  • Scott: "Is there a viable path for new solar projects today...? What's the amount of capital that's locked up in those projects that may not go forward as a potential loss? And is there a growth path currently without the tax credits?" Unidentified Company Representative: "Very little capital is locked up in the new projects... In terms of new projects, like I said, we're pausing. We're trying to figure out if there is a path forward for future projects that have a time line that goes beyond when the Big Beautiful Bill dictated that the ITC credits go away..."
  • Jim Hayden, Private Investor: "My question is around the captive insurance subsidiary. Just starting at a high level, how would you summarize performance there...?" Unidentified Company Representative: "We're very, very conservative with how we manage the cash in the captive, mostly sits in cash. We have a little bit in alternative investments. It's doing just fine."
  • Jim Hayden: "And what lines are you selling?... Is that accurate? And how are you -- what are you actually selling as far as lines of insurance?" Unidentified Company Representative: "We just started. It's still very early... but we are doing some health insurance sales, leveraging our existing marketing channels to sell some health insurance."
  • Jim Hayden: "Is there any plans to expand from that?" Unidentified Company Representative: "Yes. We're still in early stages. I don't want to say what yet, but the plan is to get into more. But to be clear, the captive insurance at this stage is not underwriting the actual insurance risk... Right now, we are acting as a broker."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts displayed a slightly negative to neutral tone, pressing on the sustainability of margin recovery and the risk of relying on weather normalization, as seen in questions about confidence in guidance and hedging strategies.
  • Management maintained a neutral to slightly defensive tone, emphasizing hedging but acknowledging limits due to "very hot end of spring and beginning of summer," using phrases such as "we think things are starting to calm down" and "we feel pretty confident."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analysts' sentiment shifted from neutral (with no Q&A in Q1) to more cautious or skeptical, while management’s tone moved from confident in prepared remarks to more defensive during the Q&A.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for consolidated adjusted EBITDA of $40 million to $50 million is unchanged from Q1.
  • The previous quarter featured strong top line and bottom line growth, while Q2 saw reduced profitability and margin pressure at GRE, despite continued revenue growth.
  • GRE customer metrics improved (higher meters served and lower churn), but margin compression due to weather and policy changes was prominent in Q2, compared to a more stable environment in Q1.
  • Management’s tone in Q2 was less confident, particularly around margin recovery, while the previous quarter featured greater optimism and less defensiveness.
  • Analyst focus in Q2 was on margin risk, hedging, and solar project viability, reflecting increased concern over external pressures and legislative changes.

Risks and Concerns

  • Significant margin compression at GRE due to wholesale power price volatility, driven by weather and policy issues.
  • Uncertainty around the impact of the recently enacted federal tax and budget legislation, particularly the "One Big Beautiful Bill," on solar project economics and pipeline viability.
  • Paused development of early-stage solar projects and halted pipeline expansion while evaluating incentive changes.
  • Analyst concerns focused on the sustainability of margin recovery, hedging effectiveness, and the viability of solar projects without tax credits.

Final Takeaway

Management signaled that while Genie Energy delivered solid operational progress and robust revenue growth in Q2, bottom line results were challenged by significant margin pressure in the retail energy unit and the evolving legislative landscape for solar incentives. The company continues to invest in growth initiatives, including solar and insurance ventures, but has paused new solar development while assessing the impact of policy changes. Despite these headwinds, Genie Energy reaffirmed its full-year adjusted EBITDA guidance, contingent on a more normalized commodity environment and continued improvement in its renewables segment.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Intel CEO reaffirms commitment to company amid Trump’s call for resignation
data.description:
Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) CEO Lip-Bu Tan issued a statement to employees on Wednesday, reaffirming his commitment to the company and its mission amid growing political scrutiny.

In the memo, Tan emphasized transparency and Intel’s continued focus on strengthening its leadership in the semiconductor industry. “There has been a lot of misinformation circulating about my past roles at Walden International and Cadence Design Systems,” he wrote. “I want to be absolutely clear: Over 40+ years in the industry, I’ve built relationships around the world and across our diverse ecosystem — and I have always operated within the highest legal and ethical standards.”

The statement follows a social media post by U.S. President Donald Trump calling for Tan’s resignation. Trump's remarks came on the heels of a letter sent by U.S. Republican Senator Tom Cotton to Intel's board chair asking questions about Tan's relations with Chinese companies and a recent criminal case involving his former company Cadence Design Systems (CDNS).

Tan exited his role as CEO of Cadence in 2021 and as board chairman in 2023. He has been CEO of Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) since March 2025.

Since then, Intel has announced a sweeping restructuring effort, including plans to cut 15% of its workforce. Tan noted that the company is “engaging with the Administration to address the matters that have been raised and ensure they have the facts.”

He also affirmed the board’s confidence in his leadership, stating: “The Board is fully supportive of the work we are doing to transform our company, innovate for our customers, and execute with discipline — and we are making progress.”

data.title: Prestige Consumer Healthcare outlines $1.1B-$1.115B FY26 revenue target amid Pillar5 acquisition and Clear Eyes supply recovery
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Prestige Consumer Healthcare Inc. (PBH) Q1 2026

Management View

  • CEO Ronald M. Lombardi stated the quarter's sales were approximately $250 million, missing the $258 million to $260 million forecast due to a planned production shutdown in eye care that lasted longer than expected, specifically affecting Clear Eyes. Lombardi highlighted, "a significant shortfall for Clear Eyes in Q1" and described actions to address this, including "the announcement to acquire Pillar5." He emphasized strong International segment growth and recovery in brands like Dramamine, Fleet, and Summer's Eve, noting that "free cash flow of $78 million was a quarterly record."

  • Lombardi announced the acquisition of Pillar5, Clear Eyes' primary supplier, as a strategic move to secure and expand long-term supply. He said, "This direct ownership will allow us to accelerate the expansion of capacity, including the start-up of a new high-speed line that we expect production from in Q3 as well as future capacity additions to fully support our expected growth in eye care demand."

  • CFO Christine Sacco reported, "Q1 revenue of $249.5 million declined 6.6% from $267.1 million in the prior year and 6.4% excluding the effects of foreign currency. EBITDA was approximately flat in Q1, and diluted EPS increased approximately 6% versus the prior year as the revenue decline was offset primarily by improved gross margin, the timing of marketing spend and lower interest expense versus the prior year."

  • Sacco added, "In the quarter, we repurchased approximately 400,000 shares for $35 million, and we'll continue to evaluate further repurchase opportunities in the remainder of fiscal '26." She also described the Pillar5 deal as "approximately neutral to EPS on a normalized basis."

Outlook

  • Management now anticipates revenues of $1.1 billion to $1.115 billion for fiscal '26, with organic revenue down approximately 1.5% to 3% versus last year. Lombardi explained this revision is "primarily driven by the anticipated eye care first half supply constraints with an additional headwind related to the current retail environment."

  • For Q2, expected revenues are approximately $256 million to $259 million, "down year-over-year, largely to Clear Eyes supply chain timing as well as lower retail order patterns experienced in July." Adjusted EPS is expected at $4.50 to $4.58 for the full year, with Q2 EPS at approximately $0.97. Free cash flow guidance remains at $245 million or more.

  • Sacco provided, "We still expect a 56.5% gross margin for the year with a Q2 gross margin of 55.5%."

Financial Results

  • Q1 revenue was $249.5 million, a 6.6% decrease from the prior year. International segment revenues grew 7.1% versus the prior year, while North America segment revenues decreased 8.4% excluding FX. Gross margin expanded to 56.2%, up 150 basis points.

  • Free cash flow reached $78 million. Net debt at June 30 was approximately $900 million with a leverage ratio of 2.4x. Interest expense was approximately $10 million, and the Q1 tax rate was about 23.2%.

  • Advertising and marketing expenses were about $35 million or 14% of sales. G&A expenses were 11.4% of sales.

Q&A

  • Rupesh Dhinoj Parikh, Oppenheimer & Co.: Asked about retail order volatility and inventory destocking. Sacco responded, "We have seen increased order volatility from retailers in July, one in particular, where we've seen big swings week-to-week in orders that are disconnected from consistent consumption levels...we are expecting to return to more normalized retail order trends in the second half."

  • Parikh asked about Clear Eyes supply normalization and market share recovery. Lombardi said, "We would anticipate that we'd be able to begin to recover that share over time...we expect to see that recover as shipments get back in."

  • Susan Kay Anderson, Canaccord Genuity: Inquired about margin impact from bringing eye care manufacturing in-house. Sacco replied, "We're not expecting any meaningful movement in our gross margin or actually any of our financial metrics."

  • Glenn West, William Blair: Sought clarity on the cadence of supply recovery. Sacco stated, "We are anticipating the third quarter and the fourth quarter to significantly step up from the first half."

  • Anthony Chester Lebiedzinski, Sidoti: Asked about the Pillar5 impact on fiscal '27. Sacco said, "We're talking about expecting it to be largely neutral to the P&L...think about this acquisition differently than a brand acquisition."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed concern over retail order volatility, supply chain recovery for Clear Eyes, and the sustainability of international growth, with a slightly negative tone regarding near-term sales headwinds and margin stability.

  • Management acknowledged disappointment with the start of the year but maintained confidence in the business fundamentals. Lombardi said, "the actions we've outlined today give us confidence for an improvement in Clear Eyes supply." Sacco emphasized a steady margin outlook and disciplined capital allocation.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone shifted from positive and growth-focused to cautious optimism, especially regarding eye care supply challenges and updated guidance. Analyst tone moved from neutral-positive to more skeptical regarding near-term performance.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for fiscal '26 was revised down from $1.140 billion-$1.155 billion in the prior quarter to $1.1 billion-$1.115 billion, with organic revenue now expected to decline versus previous growth expectations.

  • Eye care supply chain issues are now the primary driver of reduced outlook; the Pillar5 acquisition is a new strategic element.

  • International growth remains strong, but North America showed a notable revenue decline this quarter.

  • Management's confidence in margin expansion remains, but the tone has shifted to more cautious language and a focus on operational recovery.

  • Analyst questions focused more on risk mitigation, supply normalization, and capital allocation, compared to prior emphasis on innovation and channel growth.

Risks and Concerns

  • Eye care supply constraints are the central challenge, with production disruptions leading to missed sales and market share declines for Clear Eyes.

  • Retail order volatility and a "degree of expectation that what we've seen thus far will significantly impact Q2," according to Sacco.

  • Management's mitigation strategies include the Pillar5 acquisition, onboarding additional suppliers, and capital investment in supply chain capacity.

  • Tariff costs revised down to $5 million, with management expecting to offset this through cost-saving measures and pricing actions.

Final Takeaway

Prestige Consumer Healthcare highlighted a challenging start to fiscal 2026, driven by supply chain disruptions in eye care and softer retail order patterns, prompting a downward revision to revenue and EPS guidance. Management outlined decisive actions—including the Pillar5 acquisition and new supply partnerships—to restore Clear Eyes supply and position the business for recovery in the second half and beyond. Despite near-term headwinds, the company maintains steady gross margin and free cash flow guidance, underscoring continued confidence in the long-term fundamentals and capital allocation discipline.
